mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a strengthening labor market, which translates to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns as the economy recovers from a deep recession.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery phase initiated in mid-2009
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate, having been elevated above 8%, falls by more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months, it signals a definitive shift towards labor market normalization, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 9.7% as of Feb 2010, near cycle peak
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 fails to produce a positive return over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth, when the federal funds rate is constrained near the zero lower bound, indicates that economic recovery is gaining momentum without an imminent threat of monetary tightening. This combination of growth and accommodative policy drives equity prices higher.
failure_condition: The beta on INDPRO 6-month change in a return prediction model is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) when conditioned on FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery with high unemployment, an unexpected acceleration in CPI inflation raises fears of premature monetary tightening, increasing the discount rate on future earnings and causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Market is sensitive to Fed policy changes post-GFC
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month surprise in retail sales indicates stronger-than-expected consumer demand, leading to upward revisions in near-term corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically different from zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant sequential decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and construction activity, which are leading indicators of a broader economic slowdown. This negatively impacts future earnings expectations and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average falls 10% below 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market was the epicenter of the GFC
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns with greater than 50% accuracy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth indicates a significant slowdown in the broad economy, leading to downward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts across sectors and causing a broad-based decline in equity prices over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic health
failure_condition: When triggered, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held persistently below the prevailing rate of inflation (negative real rate) provides a strong incentive for capital to shift from cash and bonds into equities in search of real returns, creating a positive tailwind for the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is less than year-over-year CPIAUCSL change
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.13%, near the zero lower bound
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when the real federal funds rate is negative is not statistically significantly higher than when it is positive (t-test p > 0.05).
